top_entities.name: Fannie Mae
top_entities.news_summary: Mass termination of ~700 employees in early April 2026, with 600+ dismissed April 3-4. Majority are US citizens of Indian (Telugu-speaking) descent, over 50. FHFA Director Bill Pulte publicly called them fraudsters on Fox News; federal judge ruled Pulte immune from defamation suit under Westfall Act (first-ever such ruling for agency head, Apr 14). 66 former employees filed discrimination/defamation lawsuits alleging national-origin and age discrimination. Congressional inquiry launched by Rep. Subramanyam. Fannie Mae also axed DEI/ESG roles and is exploring public offering under FHFA pressure.
top_entities.rationale: Major negative equity/labor event April 2026: mass dismissal with ethnic concentration, public defamation by government official, active EEOC-track lawsuits, and congressional oversight. Affects EQU, EMP, ACC dimensions. Never assessed. Staleness max. F500 importance weight. Composite near 60-band boundary (65.5, within 5.5pts).

Multiple distinct uniform-score clusters identified in the fortune-500.json data: (a) Ranks 10-17: all score 4×7+3.5×1 = 81.4 — 8 entities including Cummins, Danaher, Eli Lilly, General Mills, Masimo, US Bancorp, American Express, Becton Dickinson; (b) Ranks 36-72+: most score 3.5×6+3.0×2 = 60.9 OR 3.5×8 = 62.5 — spans 30+ entities including Best Buy, Citigroup, Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, Fidelity, UPS, Delta, Gilead, Nasdaq and more; (c) Rank 1 (TIAA): 4.5×8 = 97.5.
